description:
You are a mobile build-/release-engineer who is tidying up a small internal CI pipeline repository.  
Inside the repository there is already a directory

/home/user/mobile_pipelines

with a plain–text list of build targets:

/home/user/mobile_pipelines/build_targets.txt

The file contains one target per line, each line formatted exactly as either:

android-<API_LEVEL>        (e.g. android-30)  
ios-<MAJOR_VERSION>          (e.g. ios-14)

Your assignment:

1. Read every line in /home/user/mobile_pipelines/build_targets.txt (there are exactly 6 lines when you start).  
2. Transform the list into a YAML build-matrix file called

/home/user/mobile_pipelines/build_matrix.yml

The YAML file **must** be written in the _exact_ format shown below; whitespace, indentation (2 spaces per level), field names and ordering are all important because automated tests will compare it byte-for-byte:

matrix:
  include:
    - platform: android
      api: 29
    - platform: android
      api: 30
    - platform: android
      api: 31
    - platform: ios
      version: 13
    - platform: ios
      version: 14
    - platform: ios
      version: 15

3. Create a plain-text log file called

/home/user/mobile_pipelines/transformation.log

The file must contain **exactly one** line, terminated with a single newline character, in the following format (no extra spaces):

Successfully generated build_matrix.yml with 6 entries.

Notes & constraints:

• Completing the task should require no more than 2-3 shell commands (e.g. one pipeline to create the YAML and one echo for the log).  
• Do not hard-code the entire YAML; your commands should read build_targets.txt and transform it.  
• Ensure both output files are created at the paths given above and are world-readable (the test harness runs as the same unprivileged user, so default permissions are fine).  
• Do all work from a regular, unprivileged shell—no sudo.  
• When you are finished, both /home/user/mobile_pipelines/build_matrix.yml and /home/user/mobile_pipelines/transformation.log must exist exactly as specified so that automated verification passes.
truth:
Input file that already exists before the task starts:
/home/user/mobile_pipelines/build_targets.txt
------------------------------------------------
android-29
android-30
ios-13
ios-14
android-31
ios-15

Expected file #1 produced by the agent:
/home/user/mobile_pipelines/build_matrix.yml
------------------------------------------------
matrix:
  include:
    - platform: android
      api: 29
    - platform: android
      api: 30
    - platform: android
      api: 31
    - platform: ios
      version: 13
    - platform: ios
      version: 14
    - platform: ios
      version: 15

Expected file #2 produced by the agent:
/home/user/mobile_pipelines/transformation.log
------------------------------------------------
Successfully generated build_matrix.yml with 6 entries.

(Ends with a single newline)

The automated tests will:
1. Confirm that build_matrix.yml exists at the path above and is byte-for-byte identical to the block shown here.
2. Confirm that transformation.log exists, is a single line exactly matching the string shown above (followed by a newline), and that the count “6” matches the number of entries actually found in build_matrix.yml.
No other files or modifications are checked.
